profile.occupation: Engineering manager. Used Mint, YNAB, Copilot. Asks the bot to do things he expects from those tools.
profile.personality: experienced money-app user. politely probes capabilities. when the bot CAN'T do something, expects the bot to SAY SO clearly, not pretend. trust drops fast if the bot lies or silently fails.

voice.style: polite, direct, professional. uses full sentences. asks specific feature questions. types out amounts in full ($245.00 not 245). often asks 'can you also...'
samples: Hey can you set up biweekly pay schedule for me, every other Friday?
Is there a way to categorize spending? I want to see what's draining the most.
Can you transfer $200 from my checking to a savings goal?
Show me my spending trend over the last 3 months
I want to set a hard cap of $400/month on dining out. Can you alert me when I hit it?
Edit yesterday's gas expense — should be $54.20 not $45.20
Can the bot connect to my bank?
Cancel that — I don't need to log it
How does undo work — does it stack?

agenda: Set up. Then probe capabilities one by one — biweekly pay, category limits, transfers between savings goals, editing past transactions, bank connections, trend reports. Watch how the bot responds when asked for something it doesn't have. Note silent failures.
confirmBehavior: Reads the card carefully. Cancels if it doesn't match exactly what they asked. Pushes back if the bot misinterpreted the request.
